label: spring_mass
objective: Understand equilibrium, displacement x, and restoring force F = -kx for a horizontal spring-mass system.

keyLabels.description: Displacement measured from the mean position (x = 0).
Restoring force always points toward equilibrium.
Ideal case: friction coefficient is zero, so no damping.

explainTop: Observe the dashed mean-position line through the blue reference block. The moving gray block oscillates on both sides, so the spring alternately stretches and compresses.
explainBottom: This diagram is ideal SHM on a horizontal plane. Displacement x is from equilibrium only, not total path length.

checkpointQuestions.prompt: Can you identify the instant when the block is at equilibrium (x close to 0)?
checkpointQuestions.success: Correct: at x ~ 0, restoring force magnitude is minimal and the block crosses mean position.
checkpointQuestions.hint: Watch for the block crossing the dashed mean line and the x badge approaching 0.

takeaway: x is measured from equilibrium (mean position).
Restoring force follows F = -kx and points opposite displacement.
With μ = 0, no damping is present in this ideal model.
